text:
Market today: The mood is cautious. Fed pledges to fight inflation (Warsh) and talk of the bull run ending suggest headwinds. The real driver for your book, the chip sector, faces uncertainty from trade restrictions (Nvidia H200 news).

Your holdings: SOXL (-29.19%) and SPAL (-34.25%) need immediate attention. Both are leveraged ETFs, decay over time, and are deep in the red. Trading on margin amplifies these drops significantly.

Watch: Keep a close eye on any further news regarding chip export restrictions, especially for Nvidia. Also, monitor general market sentiment for signs of a broader tech pullback.

Bottom line: Manage your leveraged, losing positions aggressively and be highly selective with any new entries in this uncertain tech environment.
plays: Review SOXL's stop at 200; consider if its -29.19% drawdown warrants re-evaluation given its leveraged nature and decay.
SPAL is a new, leveraged position down -34.25%; respect its stop at 21.38 to prevent further capital erosion.
No new buys today - hold and watch. The market signals are mixed, and chip sector news is a concern.
